text:
Anthropic introduced Claude Tag letting teams delegate work to Claude directly inside Slack across approved channels & tools.

The real winner of this shift could be $CRM as Slack becomes the coordination layer for humans and AI agents while model providers compete for distribution inside the platform.

$ZETA and $PLTR announced a strategic partnership to build marketing AI infrastructure on Palantir Foundry.

Palantir will also bring Zeta’s marketing AI tools to eligible Foundry customers creating what Zeta says could be a $100M+ annual revenue opportunity over time.

$ASTS and Rakuten plan to form a Japan joint venture in 2026 to deliver satellite broadband directly to smartphones.

The structure mirrors AST SpaceMobile’s broader carrier JV strategy and could eventually open the door for other Japanese MNOs to join the ecosystem.

$MSFT completed its first Wisconsin datacenter bringing online what it calls the world’s most powerful AI supercomputer.

The company expects to spend $4.7B in Wisconsin through 2028 with a second datacenter already under construction.

$ASTS is targeting an early August $SPCX launch for BlueBirds 11, 12, and 13.

The next batch adds larger ~2,400 sq. ft. arrays and nearly 2x peak data speeds building on Block 1’s recent 98.9 Mbps milestone.

We are reiterating Buy on $MU with a $1,500 price target as the memory cycle continues to tighten faster than expected.

We expect F3Q26 revenue of $36.7B versus $33.5B guidance driven by DRAM and NAND ASPs rising 45–50% QoQ followed by another ~25% ASP increase in F4Q26.

Longer term we see DRAM/HBM capacity staying structurally tight into 2030 supporting EPS of $153 in CY27 and $190 in CY28.
$MU DOWN 12% TODAY

$ONDS Sentrycs is collaborating with $LMT to bring its Cyber-over-RF counter-drone technology into the Sanctum C-UAS platform.

The system can detect, track & take control of unauthorized drones without jamming helping reduce disruption to nearby communications & infrastructure.

$META is launching its first smart glasses under its own brand with the $299 Adventurer and Fury models, priced $80 below the latest Ray-Ban Meta Wayfarer.

Meta is also launching a $399 Kylie Jenner Starfire model with EssilorLuxottica manufacturing the new glasses.

Deutsche Bank’s DRAM model shows demand running above supply every year through 2030.

The key driver is HBM which consumes far more wafer capacity per bit than standard DRAM.

As HBM climbs from 18% to 35% of total capacity, it tightens both HBM and standard DRAM markets at once even as $MU, Samsung and SK Hynix scale.

$WMT signed its first nuclear power agreement with $CEG for ~176MW of carbon-free electricity from Constellation’s Dresden facility in Illinois.

Another proof point that large corporations are turning to nuclear for reliable long-term power.

$SOFI launched Composer by SoFi which is an AI-powered investing platform for building, testing and automating investment strategies.

The product adds AI-driven strategy creation and backtesting to SoFi’s growing self-directed investing and wealth platform.

SoftBank’s Masayoshi Son pushed back on Elon Musk’s $SPCX orbital data center idea arguing AI race will be won by building massive compute capacity on Earth first.

Son said launch, maintenance & communication costs outweigh the power savings adding, “He who strikes first wins.”

$TSLA and NatPower announced a new $5B battery storage initiative across Italy and the United Kingdom.

The first five projects will be built across both markets, with the total program targeting more than 100 GWh of storage capacity.

U.S. equities are at record highs, rate cuts are being pushed further out, and AI-driven gains are concentrated in a narrow set of companies.

For investors fully exposed to U.S. equities, the question is no longer just about upside.

It's about what happens if sentiment shifts.

Join KraneShares CIO @ahern_brendan, @Hedgeye CEO @KeithMcCullough, and @HedgeyeAM CIO John McNamara III for a discussion on how to stay invested in U.S. equities while seeking to manage downside risk through today's elevated market environment.

The conversation will cover how $KSPY's dynamic, options-based approach seeks to offer a more responsive hedge than traditional buffer strategies, and how it has performed through recent volatility.

Global semiconductor sales could rise 90% YoY to $1.51 trillion, per the WSTS, a substantial revision from its prior forecast for $975 billion. 

Memory is expected to be the primary driver, up 250% to $803.9 billion, or more than 53% of the market. 

$NVDA $AMD $AVGO $MU

For comparison, the EPA estimates Americans use 3.3 trillion gallons of water each year to water lawns, meaning Amazon’s data center usage is less than 0.1% of that. 

$AMZN $NVDA $GOOG

OpenAI reportedly spent $34 billion last year, with $19 billion of that going towards R&D and roughly $6 billion going towards marketing, per the FT. 

$MSFT $AMZN $GOOG

Nvidia has become the number one vendor by revenue in the data center Ethernet switching market, per IDC, with the overall market growing 39.8% YoY in Q1 to $15.4 billion.

$NVDA $AVGO

📺 THE CHARTS DIDN'T SUPPORT BEARS YESTERDAY — WHAT CHANGED? + $MU WARNING + $TSLA WORTH WATCHING? + $SPCX NEW SETUP

Yesterday's charts didn't support a bearish thesis, but that has changed.

The market had been flashing subtle warning signs for days. Most stocks were already trading below their 8- and 21-day moving averages; software names had been deteriorating for weeks; big-cap tech names like $AMZN were below their 200-day moving average; and leadership had narrowed dramatically. 

The indexes still looked okay on the surface, but underneath, breadth was weakening, and very few stocks were acting well.

*

Then came the technical triggers. 

Both $SPY and $QQQ pushed above prior highs and failed to hold them, creating classic Red Dog Reversal sell signals. 

The market also broke lower from an inside wedge pattern, confirming that the risk-reward had shifted. 

Monday's early action offered traders a chance to reduce exposure, raise cash, and even establish some short positions before the larger decline unfolded.

*

This wasn't a signal to "short the world." It was a signal to get more defensive. 

I repeatedly stress the importance of sell rules, respecting stops, trimming extended positions, and adapting when market conditions change.

These are the rules that keep traders in the game when the tape suddenly turns.

I am not completely out of the market. I still have some long exposure through calls, a few remaining positions, and short premium trades. But I have significantly reduced risk and believe cash has become a valuable position once the warning signs started to stack up.

*

Key levels now matter.

For $QQQ, $720 has become important resistance after being lost, with $711 as the next downside area to watch and potentially much lower levels if selling accelerates. 

For $SPY, $735 is near-term support while the 50-day moving average around $729 remains the more significant line in the sand.

*

We have $MU earnings tomorrow. Micron serves as a reminder that even strong stocks can become vulnerable when they get too extended from short-term moving averages. 

I hope you had a chance to trim and take some profits. Tomorrow is the key not only for the stock, but for the whole market.

*

Among individual names, $TSLA stands out as one worth watching. 

While not a leadership stock yet, it showed relative strength compared to much of the market, attracted buyers yesterday, and has occasionally moved higher when the broader tape struggles. 

I'll be watching whether buyers defend the recent lows.

*

$SPCX is back in my focus as well.

After breaking below important support levels, the stock is opening under $150. 

A reclaim of $150 could create a tactical trade toward $154, but if it remains below that level, there may be little support until much closer to the IPO price.

*

So, the market didn't suddenly become bearish overnight. The evidence simply changed.

Failed breakouts, weakening breadth, deteriorating leadership, and broken support levels have shifted the tone from a confidently bullish to a cautiously defensive one.

The charts didn't support bears yesterday, but today, they're giving them a lot more evidence.

*

If you found this helpful, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet
